learning_outcomes.description: Construct logically correct and mathematically sound proofs.

Apply concepts of logic, set theory, relations, induction, principles of counting, probability, algebraic structures and elementary number theory to mathematical problems in more advanced mathematical courses and to problems encountered in other fields of study.
Demonstrate an understanding and well-founded knowledge of the mathematics presented in this course and thus be able to apply techniques from this course to solve both familiar and novel problems.
Select and use the appropriate level, style and means of written communication, using the symbolic, graphical, and diagrammatic forms relevant to the context.
Understand some applications of mathematics to relevant fields, such as computer programming and logic.

assessments.weighting: <dd>30% The best 8 out of 12 will count for assessment. </dd>

The exercises are to be completed during the weekly Applied Classes, except in Week 1. If you cannot attend your scheduled applied class, you may attend another applied class that week. Information about all available applied classes will be provided on the course website.
assessments.task_description: The exercises are to be completed during the weekly applied classes, except in Week 1. Students may discuss the exercises with other students during the applied class. The use of AI tools or other electronic assistance is not permitted.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit your work in person during an Applied Class. You may attend any applied class, not only the one listed on your timetable.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: If you miss your own Applied Class session, then you can join any other MATH1061 Applied Class session that week (a schedule will be provided on the course website). There is no possibility for submissions outside of that week.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Pre-class videos, readings, and quizzes

To prepare for each class, you will need to watch one or two short videos, read some pages of the textbook, and complete a short quiz.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Lectures

Each week there will be three hours of lectures during which you will discuss important concepts, work through examples, and answer questions.

learning_activities.topic_description: Lectures Each week there will be three hours of lectures during which you will discuss important concepts, work through examples, and answer questions.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 13(03 Aug - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Applied Classes

Applied classes give students a chance to work through assignments and ask questions relating to both assessment pieces and material covered in lectures. Applied classes also give students the opportunity to discuss with their peers approaches to formulating and solving mathematical problems.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
